title: Yacht Rental in Nice: Costs, Seasons and Tips 2026
html:
Yacht rental in Nice gives you the French Riviera from the water, with most charters leaving Port de Nice or the marina at Villefranche-sur-Mer. Day prices typically run from about EUR 600 for a small motorboat to several thousand for a crewed yacht, and BoatHire24 lists boats for every budget.

Where to set off in Nice

Nice sits on the Baie des Anges, and the coastline around it is dense with sheltered harbours and anchorages. Most skippers point west toward Cap d'Antibes or east toward Monaco, both comfortable day runs. The water is generally calm in the mornings, which suits an early departure.

  • Port de Nice (Lympia): the historic harbour beneath the old town, the most common pick-up point for charters.
  • Villefranche-sur-Mer: a deep, protected bay a short hop east, popular for swimming stops.
  • Cap Ferrat: the wooded peninsula with quiet coves between Villefranche and Beaulieu.
  • Beaulieu-sur-Mer: a small marina with easy access to the Corniche coastline.
  • Cap d'Antibes: roughly 12 nautical miles west, a classic anchorage for lunch on board.
  • Monaco: about 8 nautical miles east, a frequent turnaround point for full-day trips.

Which boat to rent in Nice?

The right boat depends on your group size, your budget and whether you want a skipper. Smaller motorboats suit couples and short coastal hops, while larger yachts make sense for a full day with friends or family. Below are the most popular options on the Riviera.

Nice charters range from open day-boats to fully crewed yachts, so it helps to match the vessel to your plan before you compare prices.

  1. Open motorboat best for short coastal cruising and swimming stops, seats up to 6, from EUR 600 per day.
  2. RIB (rigid inflatable) best for fast hops to Monaco or Antibes, seats up to 8, from EUR 700 per day.
  3. Sailing yacht best for a relaxed full day under canvas, seats up to 8, from EUR 900 per day.
  4. Catamaran best for stable space and shade for groups, seats up to 12, from EUR 1,500 per day.
  5. Crewed motor yacht best for comfort and range with a skipper, seats up to 10, from EUR 3,000 per day. For a sense of the class, this motor yacht this motor yacht shows the kind of crewed listing available here.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Nice?

A day on the water in Nice can cost anywhere from about EUR 60 per seat on a shared tour to several thousand euros for a private crewed yacht. The final figure depends on the boat size, the season and whether fuel and a skipper are included. You can compare current rates and browse boats for your dates to see live availability before you book.

  • A shared group tour from Nice typically costs from about EUR 60 to EUR 90 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day rents from roughly EUR 350 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day starts at around EUR 1,500 in peak season.
  • A crewed yacht with a skipper begins near EUR 3,000 per day, fuel usually extra.

When is the best season to charter in Nice?

The high season on the Riviera runs from June to September, when the sea is warm and settled and demand peaks. May and early October are quieter and often cheaper, with pleasant water and fewer crowds. Booking ahead in 2026 is wise for July and August dates.

Local tip: the Mistral, a cold north-westerly wind, can build quickly in the afternoons and kick up a choppy sea off Nice, so many skippers leave early and return by mid-afternoon. France generally requires the person at the helm of a powered yacht to hold a recognised boating licence, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat before you sail.

At a glance

  • Price band: from about EUR 350 half-day to EUR 3,000+ per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: typically 4 hours half-day or 8 hours full day
  • Capacity: commonly 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (euro)
  • Nearest hotspots: Monaco about 8 nautical miles east, Cap d'Antibes about 12 nautical miles west

Questions about boat rental in Nice

Do I need a licence to rent a yacht in Nice?

For skippered or crewed charters no licence is needed because a qualified captain runs the boat. For self-drive powered boats France generally requires a recognised licence, and the operator confirms the precise rules for each vessel before departure.

How many people can join a yacht charter in Nice?

Capacity varies by boat, with small motorboats taking up to 6 guests and catamarans or larger yachts seating 10 to 12. The listing states the maximum for each vessel, so check it against your group before you book.

How long is a typical day charter from Nice?

A half-day charter usually lasts about 4 hours and a full day about 8 hours, leaving time to reach Monaco or Cap d'Antibes, swim, and return before the afternoon wind builds along the Baie des Anges.

Is fuel included in the price?

Fuel is sometimes included and sometimes charged on top, especially for larger motor yachts that burn more. Each listing sets out what the rate covers, so read the inclusions carefully before confirming your dates.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Nice or list your own, so whether you want a single day on the Baie des Anges or a crewed week along the Riviera, you can browse the listings, and boat owners in Nice are welcome to add their vessels too.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Nice and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
